headline: HEAVY SLOP WARNING: PRE-INDEPENDENCE PYROTECHNIC FRONT BLANKETS THE FACEBOOK DELTA AS SYNTHETIC FIREWORKS DISPLAYS DETONATE OVER TOWNS THAT ARE NOT HOSTING ANY
advisory: A dense holiday-eve slop system has developed across the Facebook Delta and adjacent shorelines, driven by seasonal engagement demand and near-limitless synthetic image capacity. Residents are advised that a majority of circulating 'tonight's fireworks over [YOUR TOWN]' images depict celebrations that will not occur, cannot occur, and in several cases violate the ordinary behavior of light. The Service reminds the public that municipal fireworks schedules are still published, drably, by municipalities. Verify before you reshare.

regional_outlook.conditions: Saturated. Expect wall-to-wall reposts of impossibly grand fireworks over local landmarks, each captioned to a different town. Comment sections filling with 'gorgeous!' from accounts created this week.
Heavy accumulation of 'patriotic tablescape' inspiration boards where the flags have too many stripes and the pies defy geometry. Pinning at your own risk.
Rising tide of drop-ship 'handmade' flag bunting rendered by machine and photographed in rooms that do not exist. Shipping estimates arrive July 8th, which is a tell.
Queries for 'local fireworks near me' returning confidently fabricated event times and phantom parking instructions. Ground truth remains available two pages down, dampened.
Looping AI grill-and-firework montages set to royalty-free anthems. Sparks cast no light and the burgers do not cook, yet the algorithm remains enthused.

specimen.status: Seasonal migrant; abundant in the 72 hours preceding any national holiday.
specimen.habitat: Local community groups, event pages, and civic-pride feeds across the Facebook Delta, where regional loyalty is dense and verification is thin.
specimen.field_marks: Blooms of fireworks larger than the buildings beneath them; onlookers with surplus fingers; symmetrical crowds all facing identical angles; brilliant reflections on pavement that was never wet. Sparks illuminate nothing.
specimen.call: 🎆 You won't BELIEVE the fireworks in OUR town tonight! 🇺🇸 Share to spread the pride before it's gone! 👇
specimen.confusion_species: An actual photograph of a real fireworks display. Tell them apart by looking at the crowd's hands, checking whether the caption names one specific town or 'YOUR town,' and asking whether the poster has ever set foot in the county.  Genuine civic pride usually includes a road-closure complaint.

spotter_tip: If a stunning local fireworks photo is being shared with the exact same words in fourteen different town groups, it is one system, not fourteen celebrations. Cross-reference the caption against the actual municipal calendar — the boring PDF is the real one.
tomorrow: Independence Day peak: severe display farming likely nationwide, with scattered fake veterans requesting a 'salute in the comments.' Salute a real one instead.
